one_time_description: Kahkewistahaw Mismanagement Claim Settlement of $49,403,870 received, with related distributions including $35,000,000 contribution to Legacy Trust, $2,470,000 legal fees, and $4,150,000 per capita distribution
economic_notes: First Nation operates Kahkewistahaw Birds Point Resort Limited Partnership, Weyotisiwin Holdings Limited Partnership, Kahkewistahaw Development Limited Partnership, Kahkewistahaw Management Limited Partnership. Kahkewistahaw Economic Development (combined) had total revenues of $10,878,434 and net income of $623,381 for period ended December 31, 2019. Penipa Hotel Limited Partnership is 100% owned by Kahkewistahaw Development Limited Partnership. Flood claim initiated against Government of Canada for damages.
notes: Fiscal year 2020 (not 2024). Large one-time Mismanagement Claim Settlement of $49,403,870 received with offsetting distributions. Complex consolidation including 6 consolidated entities and 1 GBE (Kahkewistahaw Economic Development combined) using modified equity method. Kahkewistahaw 1907 Specific Claim Trust, Legacy Trust, Community Development Account excluded as not controlled by Chief and Council. FNFA 30-year debenture of $14,587,230 issued during year converting from interim financing. Flood claim against Government of Canada is a contingent asset. Long-term debt includes capital lease obligations of $389,784 and FNFA interim financing of $3,237,640. total_government_transfers calculated as ISC $12,517,496 + FNIHB $638,638 + CMHC $641,241 + FNIHB Home Care $174,981 = $13,972,356 (Enbridge grants $254,200 and First Nations Trust $623,725 also possible government transfers bringing total to approx $14,850,281 - reported conservatively excluding Enbridge and FN Trust). OSR calculated as total revenue minus government transfers.
